attractions.name: American Visionary Art Museum (AVAM) AVAM
attractions.description: The American Visionary Art Museum (AVAM) is America's official national museum, education center, and repository for self-taught and intuitive artistry. It specializes in original thematic exhibitions that combine art, science, philosophy, humor, and social justice. The museum champions 'outsider' artists, including farmers, housewives, and the homeless, all inspired by their own inner creative fire. AVAM, Visit Baltimore
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM AVAM
attractions.admission: Adult: $20, Senior (60+): $17, Active Military/Veterans (with ID): $11, Child/Student (with ID): $11, Children 6 & under: Free AVAM
highlights: 'Iconic mirrored mosaic facade created by at-risk Baltimore youth [Visit Baltimore', 'Vollis Simpson’s Giant Whirligig located in the wildflower garden AVAM', "Andrew Logan’s hypnotic, eight-foot mirrored 'Cosmic Egg' Visit Baltimore", 'The Jim Rouse Visionary Center, which houses large-scale works and automata Wikipedia', "Sideshow, the museum's eccentric and award-winning gift shop Visit Baltimore", 'The annual East Coast Championship Kinetic Sculpture Race Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Bank of America cardholders can receive free admission during the first full weekend of each month. For free transportation, take the Charm City Circulator (Banner Route) to stop 404. Metered street parking and a public lot are available on Key Highway. Visit Baltimore, Let's Go To The Museum
attractions.nearby: Federal Hill Park, Inner Harbor, National Aquarium, Maryland Science Center Visit Baltimore

attractions.name: B&O Railroad Museum (B&O Railroad Museum)
attractions.description: The B&O Railroad Museum is recognized as the birthplace of American railroading, situated on the historic grounds of the Mount Clare Shops. It houses the oldest and most comprehensive collection of railroad artifacts in the Western Hemisphere, featuring an unparalleled roster of 19th and 20th-century locomotives and rolling stock (B&O Railroad Museum, Tripadvisor).
attractions.address: 901 West Pratt Street, Baltimore, MD 21223 (B&O Railroad Museum)
attractions.hours: Daily: 10 AM - 4 PM (B&O Railroad Museum)
attractions.admission: Adults: $20, Seniors (60+): $17, Children (2-12): $12, B&O Members: Free. Discounts: $1 for Museums For All (EBT/SNAP), Free for Active Duty Military, Teachers, and 100% Disability Rating Veterans (B&O Railroad Museum)
highlights: 'Mount Clare Roundhouse and its soaring cupola ([Visit Maryland)', 'Extensive collection of early American locomotives and rolling stock (B&O Railroad Museum)', "Seasonal train rides on the nation's first mile of commercial track (B&O Railroad Museum)", 'Hays T. Watkins Research Library and archives (B&O Railroad Museum)', 'On-site Restoration Facility for historic rail equipment (B&O Railroad Museum)']
attractions.tips: Admission does not include train rides; tickets for rides must be purchased on-site. Free off-street parking is available on the museum grounds. Check the online calendar for $10 admission days on the second Wednesday of the month (B&O Railroad Museum)
attractions.nearby: Irish Railroad Workers Museum, Inner Harbor (National Aquarium, Maryland Science Center), and Fort McHenry (Irish American Museum, Lord Baltimore Hotel)

attractions.name: Baltimore Convention Center
attractions.description: The Baltimore Convention Center is the Mid-Atlantic region's premier venue for conventions, meetings, trade shows, and special events, featuring 300,000 square feet of exhibition space. Since opening in 1979, it has served as a cornerstone of economic growth and tourism, helping catalyze the development of Baltimore's Inner Harbor. Official Website
attractions.address: One West Pratt Street, Baltimore, Maryland 21201-2466
attractions.hours: General Information: Monday - Friday 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM; 24/7 Security; Event hours vary by schedule. Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily (Monday - Sunday) during events; Office: Monday - Friday
attractions.admission: Admission varies by event; free for some public events, ticketed for others (e.g., historical average ~$48-$85 for paid conventions). Many events offer free admission for children 10 and under. Vivid Seats, Baltimore Comic-Con
highlights: ['300,000 square feet of contiguous exhibition space', '85,000 square feet of flexible meeting space across 50 rooms', '36,672-square-foot multifunctional ballroom with specialized lighting', 'Enclosed skywalk bridge connecting to the 752-room Hilton Baltimore hotel', 'Gold-level sustainable event certification through the Events Industry Council', "Direct access to the Maryland Transit Administration's light rail system"]
attractions.tips: The venue is cashless for many services; advance parking reservations via SpotHero are recommended. Take advantage of the free Charm City Circulator for local transit, and check for nursing stations and ADA accessibility coordinators if needed. Official Website, EXHIBITOR Online
attractions.nearby: Inner Harbor, Oriole Park at Camden Yards, National Aquarium, Maryland Science Center, Bromo Seltzer Arts Tower, CFG Bank Arena, American Visionary Art Museum. TripAdvisor

attractions.name: Baltimore Police Museum
attractions.description: The Baltimore Police Museum, located on the ground floor of the Bishop L. Robinson, Sr. Police Administration Building, honors the history and traditions of the Baltimore Police Department since its founding in 1784. It serves as both an educational institution and a memorial, showcasing the evolution of law enforcement professionals through extensive collections and exhibits (Baltimore Police Department).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: Closed; Friday: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM (by appointment); Sunday: Closed (Baltimore Police Department)
attractions.admission: Free (Baltimore Police Department, Care.com)
highlights: '1847 Desk Sergeant’s logbook from the Southern District ([WMAR 2 News)', 'Collection of vintage Baltimore police vehicles and equipment (Baltimore Police Museum)', 'Authentic old jail cell exhibit (WMAR 2 News)', 'Comprehensive archive of Baltimore Police Academy graduation photos (Baltimore Police History)', "Memorabilia and artifacts dating back to the department's 18th-century origins (Baltimore Police Department)", 'Memorial dedicated to fallen officers of the Baltimore Police Department (Baltimore Police Department)']
attractions.tips: Public access is primarily on Fridays; Saturday visits require a prior appointment via the online request form. As the museum is located within a secure police administration building, visitors should be prepared for security screening upon entry (Baltimore Police Department).
attractions.nearby: Port Discovery Children's Museum (Port Discovery), Historic Ships in Baltimore (Historic Ships), Reginald F. Lewis Museum (Visit Baltimore), National Great Blacks In Wax Museum (Visit Baltimore)

attractions.name: Baltimore's Inner Harbor (Visit Baltimore)
attractions.category: Landmark, Entertainment District, Tourist Attraction (Visit Baltimore)
attractions.description: Baltimore's Inner Harbor is a historic seaport and landmark that serves as the city's central hub for tourism and entertainment. Once a center for shipbuilding and steel mills, it has been transformed into a scenic waterfront destination featuring world-class museums, fresh seafood restaurants, and family-friendly activities. (Visit Baltimore)
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Thursday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Friday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Saturday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Sunday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM (Waterfront Promenade) (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free for general access; individual attractions such as the National Aquarium and Maryland Science Center have separate admission fees (Visit Baltimore), (Sunshine Whispers)
highlights: 'National Aquarium: Home to more than 20,000 animals in award-winning habitats ([Visit Baltimore)', 'Maryland Science Center: Features three floors of hands-on science demonstrations and exhibits (Visit Baltimore)', 'Historic Ships in Baltimore: A collection of floating museums, including the Civil War-era USS Constellation (Visit Baltimore)', 'Top of the World Observation Level: Located on the 27th floor of the World Trade Center, offering panoramic city views (Visit Baltimore)', "Port Discovery Children’s Museum: Ranked among the nation’s best children's museums with interactive play areas (Visit Baltimore)", 'Chessie Dragon Paddle Boats: A fun and iconic way to explore the harbor waters (Visit Baltimore)']
attractions.tips: Visit the Baltimore Visitor Center at 401 Light Street for personalized advice and local maps. Use the Baltimore Water Taxi for a scenic and efficient way to travel between waterfront neighborhoods. Check for seasonal hours and special events, such as concerts at Pier Six Pavilion, during your visit. (Visit Baltimore)
attractions.nearby: Federal Hill Park, Fort McHenry National Monument, Fells Point, Little Italy (Expedia), (Visit Baltimore)

attractions.name: Druid Hill Park
attractions.description: Druid Hill Park is a 745-acre urban oasis in the heart of Baltimore, known for its shady lawns, rolling hills, and majestic forests. Inaugurated in 1860, it is one of the oldest landscaped public parks in the United States and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. (Visit Baltimore, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Daily, sunrise to sunset (Birder's Guide to Maryland and DC)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Birder's Guide to Maryland and DC)
attractions.admission: Free (individual attractions like the Maryland Zoo and Conservatory have separate entry fees) (Visit Baltimore, Birder's Guide to Maryland and DC)
highlights: ['The Maryland Zoo in Baltimore', 'Howard Peters Rawlings Conservatory & Botanic Gardens', 'Druid Hill Reservoir and Lakeside Loop Trail', '18-hole disc golf course', 'Historic pavilions and monuments (e.g., Moorish Tower, Chinese Pavilion)', 'Baltimore Model Safety City']
attractions.tips: The park is popular for jogging and biking, especially around the reservoir. Visit the Rawlings Conservatory for beautiful plant displays and the seasonal Farmers' Market on Wednesdays from June to September. (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia)
attractions.nearby: Woodberry Park, Roosevelt Park, Mondawmin Mall, Jones Falls Trail (Baltimore City Recreation & Parks)

attractions.name: Edgar Allan Poe House and Museum
attractions.description: The Edgar Allan Poe House and Museum is a historic row home where American writer Edgar Allan Poe lived from 1833 to 1835. This National Historic Landmark is preserved with much of its original 1830s interior and features exhibits on Poe’s life, family, and literary career in Baltimore.
attractions.hours: Thursday – Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Weekly); Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM (May – October). Last entry at 3:15 PM.
attractions.admission: Adults: $10.00, Seniors: $8.00, Children (3-12): $6.00. Timed entry reservations are required. Source: Edgar Allan Poe House & Museum, Source: WhichMuseum
highlights: ['Designated as a National Historic Landmark in 1972 and a Literary Landmark in 2020.', 'Preserved 1830s architecture where Poe lived with his aunt, grandmother, and cousins.', 'Displays of significant artifacts including Poe’s lap desk, chair, and telescope.', 'Home to the annual International Edgar Allan Poe Festival & Awards held each October.', 'Exhibits featuring the life of Poe’s wife Virginia Clemm and her death portrait.']
attractions.tips: Reservations are required as the historic site is very small. The house features extremely steep, narrow stairs and is not ADA accessible. There are no public restrooms available on-site.
attractions.nearby: Westminster Hall & Burying Ground (Poe's Grave), Carroll Mansion, Phoenix Shot Tower, B&O Railroad Museum, Enoch Pratt Free Library Poe Room

attractions.name: Fire Museum of Maryland (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Fire Museum of Maryland is one of the largest fire museums in the United States, showcasing over 250 years of urban firefighting history. Founded in 1971, the museum houses a world-class collection of more than 40 historic apparatus, including hand engines, steam-powered vehicles, and motorized trucks. It serves as a leading institution for preserving and interpreting the evolution of the fire service while teaching safety and emergency preparedness to all ages (Official Website, Visit Maryland).
attractions.hours: Saturdays: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Year-round); June – August: Wednesday – Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Saturdays year-round; Wednesdays through Saturdays from June to August (Official Website, Summer Schedule)
attractions.admission: Adults: $15, Seniors & Firefighters: $13, Children (Ages 2-17): $7, Children (Under 2): FREE, Members: FREE (Official Website)
highlights: 'Extensive collection of over 40 historic fire apparatus, including hand-pumped engines and steam-powered pumpers ([Official Website)', 'The Great Baltimore Fire of 1904 exhibit, detailing one of the most significant urban fires in American history (Official Website)', 'National Fire Control Center exhibit featuring a 1930s-style dispatch room and alarm systems (Official Website)', 'The historic fireboat Thomas D’Alesandro Jr., which patrolled the Chesapeake Bay for over 50 years (National Park Service)', 'Discovery Room for children, offering hands-on activities and the opportunity to try on fire gear (Tripadvisor)']
attractions.tips: Plan to spend about 1-2 hours to fully explore the exhibits. Check the museum's event calendar for special summer activities like Story Time on Fridays and Fire Safety Fun on Wednesdays. Free parking is available on-site (Official Website, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Maryland State Fairgrounds, Loch Raven Reservoir, Towson Town Center (Expedia, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Harborplace
attractions.description: Harborplace is a landmark festival marketplace located in the heart of Baltimore's Inner Harbor, currently undergoing a major revitalization project. The destination features two iconic waterfront pavilions—the Pratt Street Pavilion and the Light Street Pavilion—which host a diverse mix of local vendors, eateries, and cultural installations. It serves as a central hub for community engagement, waterfront dining, and shopping while the site transition to a reimagined mixed-use space. (Our Harborplace, Explore Baltimore Heritage)
attractions.address: 201 East Pratt Street and 301 Light Street
attractions.hours: Daily 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM (Individual tenant hours may vary; restaurants often stay open later) (Baltimore Fishbowl, Our Harborplace Events)
attractions.admission: Free (Our Harborplace)
highlights: ['Waterfront dining at the Pratt Street and Light Street Pavilions', 'Local Baltimore-made goods and artisanal shops', 'Scenic views of the Inner Harbor and Chesapeake Bay', 'Community events and seasonal live music at the Amphitheater', 'Cultural art installations featuring local artists', 'Proximity to historic ships and maritime landmarks']
attractions.tips: Parking is available at nearby garages like the Pier V Garage or the Renaissance Hotel. Visit during the summer for free Sunday concert series at the amphitheater, and check the official website for current 'reimagining' updates and pop-up vendor events. (Our Harborplace, Pier V Garage)
attractions.nearby: National Aquarium, Historic Ships in Baltimore, Maryland Science Center, Port Discovery Children's Museum, Top of the World Observation Level (Visit Baltimore)

attractions.name: Historic Ships in Baltimore (formerly Baltimore Maritime Museum)
attractions.description: Historic Ships in Baltimore is a maritime museum that manages a collection of historic vessels and sites in Baltimore's Inner Harbor. It serves as a vital institution for preserving and interpreting over 200 years of Maryland's naval and maritime heritage through its unique fleet of ships. Historic Ships in Baltimore Mission
attractions.hours: Daily 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (April 4 – Labor Day). Seasonal changes apply; check website for winter and holiday schedules. Historic Ships in Baltimore Hours
attractions.days_open: Daily (April 4 – Labor Day); Schedule varies seasonally. Historic Ships in Baltimore Hours
attractions.admission: Adult (21–59): $21.95, Senior (60+)/Military/Student (15–20): $19.95, Youth (6–14): $9.95, Child (under 5) & Members: Free. Historic Ships in Baltimore Tickets
highlights: 'USS Constellation: The last sail-only warship designed and built by the U.S. Navy. [Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'USS Torsk: A World War II-era Tench-class submarine that patrolled Japanese waters. Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'USCG Cutter 37: One of the last warships afloat that was present during the attack on Pearl Harbor. Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'Lightship 116 Chesapeake: An essential navigation aid that guided traffic along the Chesapeake Bay. Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'Seven Foot Knoll Lighthouse: A historic 1856 screw-pile lighthouse with panoramic harbor views. Historic Ships in Baltimore']
attractions.tips: Tickets are 'Boarding Passes' valid until used and can be used on separate days for different ships. Note that the museum and gift shops are cashless and only accept credit/debit cards. Visitors under 17 must be accompanied by an adult. Historic Ships in Baltimore Visit
attractions.nearby: National Aquarium, Maryland Science Center, Harborplace Shopping & Dining, Port Discovery Children's Museum. Visit Baltimore

attractions.name: Lexington Market
attractions.description: Lexington Market is a historic public market in Downtown Baltimore, established in 1782 and currently housed in a modern 60,000-square-foot facility completed in 2022. It is the oldest continuously operating public market in America, featuring over 50 merchants offering a diverse range of local food, produce, and merchandise. Lexington Market, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Mon - Wed: 6:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thu - Fri: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sat: 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sun: Closed Lexington Market
attractions.admission: Free Lexington Market
highlights: 'Oldest continuously operating public market in the United States, founded in 1782 [Lexington Market', 'Newly renovated 60,000-square-foot market shed completed in 2022 Wikipedia', 'Home to over 50 local merchants and food kiosks Lexington Market', 'Features significant works of public art on the plaza and inside the hall Lexington Market', 'Located in the Bromo Arts & Entertainment District Lexington Market']
attractions.tips: Visit early for the freshest selection from merchants. Be sure to check individual merchant pages as their specific hours may differ from the general market hours. Take advantage of the West Market Garage for convenient parking. Lexington Market
attractions.nearby: Edgar Allan Poe's Gravesite & Memorial, Hippodrome Theatre, Everyman Theatre, University of Maryland - Baltimore, Bromo Seltzer Tower, Camden Yards Lexington Market

attractions.name: Maryland Science Center Maryland Science Center
attractions.description: The Maryland Science Center is an interactive science museum featuring three floors of hands-on exhibits, a planetarium, and an IMAX theater. Located in Baltimore's Inner Harbor, it offers engaging programs for children and families that explore physical science, space, and the human body. Maryland Science Center, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 601 Light Street, Baltimore, MD 21230 Maryland Science Center
attractions.hours: Mon: Closed; Tue-Fri: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sat-Sun: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM Maryland Science Center
attractions.admission: Adult: $29.95, Child (Ages 3-12): $21.95, Senior (Age 62+): $28.95, Member Adults & Children: FREE, Access Science (qualifying families): $5.00, IMAX Add-on: +$5 Maryland Science Center
highlights: 'Dinosaur Mysteries featuring more than two dozen skeletons [Wikipedia', 'Davis Planetarium with star-studded shows Maryland Science Center', 'Five-story IMAX Theater showing 3D documentaries Maryland Science Center', 'Crossey Ramsey Memorial Observatory for stargazing Wikipedia', 'Newton’s Alley interactive physics exhibit Yelp', 'Live science demonstrations on the Science Demo Stage Maryland Science Center']
attractions.tips: Add an IMAX documentary to your ticket for just $5 more upon arrival. Visit the rooftop observatory on Friday nights for free telescope viewings if weather permits. Use the free Charm City Circulator shuttle to reach the center and avoid limited street parking. Maryland Science Center, Expedia
attractions.nearby: National Aquarium, American Visionary Art Museum, Federal Hill Park, Port Discovery Children's Museum Visit Baltimore, Wikipedia

attractions.name: National Aquarium
attractions.description: The National Aquarium is a premier nonprofit public aquarium located in the heart of Baltimore's Inner Harbor (National Aquarium). It houses more than 17,000 animals across award-winning exhibits such as the Blacktip Reef and a multi-story Atlantic Coral Reef (Wikipedia)).
attractions.address: 501 E. Pratt St., Baltimore, MD 21202 (National Aquarium)
attractions.hours: Monday-Thursday: 9 am – 6 pm; Friday: 9 am – 9 pm; Saturday: 9 am – 7 pm; Sunday: 9 am – 6 pm (National Aquarium)
attractions.admission: Adults (21-69): $49.95; Youth (5-20): $39.95; Seniors (70+): $39.95; Children (4 & under): Free (National Aquarium)
highlights: 'Blacktip Reef ([National Aquarium)', 'Harbor Wetland (National Aquarium)', 'Upland Tropical Rain Forest (National Aquarium)', 'Atlantic Coral Reef (National Aquarium)', 'Shark Alley (National Aquarium)', 'Dolphin Discovery (National Aquarium)']
attractions.tips: Buy tickets online in advance to ensure entry; strollers are not allowed on exhibit paths but parking is provided; lockers are available for a small fee; last entry is 60 minutes before closing (National Aquarium).
attractions.nearby: USS Torsk, Lightship Chesapeake, Maryland Science Center, Port Discovery Children’s Museum (TripAdvisor, Visit Baltimore)

attractions.name: Nick's Fish House
attractions.description: Nick's Fish House is a premier waterfront dining destination located in the Baltimore Peninsula, offering a wide selection of fresh seafood and regional specialties. The restaurant is particularly famous for its hot steamed Eastern Shore crabs and jumbo lump crab cakes, served in a vibrant atmosphere with scenic views of the Patapsco River (Nick's Fish House, Baltimore Blueway).
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM (Nick's Fish House)
attractions.admission: Free entry; menu prices vary (Nick's Fish House)
highlights: "Hot steamed Eastern Shore crabs prepared with Old Bay seasoning ([Nick's Fish House)", "Famous jumbo lump crab cakes and regional seafood specialties (Nick's Fish House)", 'Spacious waterfront deck with scenic views of the Patapsco River (Tripadvisor)', "Live music and entertainment on the deck from Thursday through Sunday (Nick's Fish House)", "Signature hand-squeezed orange crushes and craft beer selections (Nick's Fish House)", "Year-round service for lunch, dinner, and happy hour (Nick's Fish House)"]
attractions.tips: Visit during sunset for the best waterfront views and check the live music schedule for weekend afternoon entertainment (Oreate). Be prepared for a messy but rewarding experience when ordering the steamed crabs; mallets and picking instructions are provided (Nick's Fish House).
attractions.nearby: Sagamore Spirit Distillery, Port Covington Marina, Under Armour Global Headquarters (Port Covington Marina)

attractions.name: Port Discovery Children's Museum
attractions.description: Port Discovery Children's Museum provides unique opportunities and high-quality experiences that make play accessible, educational, and fun for all. Located in the historic Fish Market building in Baltimore's Inner Harbor, the museum features three floors of interactive exhibits and programs designed specifically for children from birth through age 10 (Port Discovery, Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9 AM - 3 PM; Tuesday: CLOSED; Wednesday: CLOSED; Thursday: 10 AM - 3 PM; Friday: 10 AM - 5 PM; Saturday: 10 AM - 5 PM; Sunday: 11 AM - 5 PM (Port Discovery)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesday-Wednesday during Winter/Spring 2026) (Port Discovery)
attractions.admission: Non-Member (Ages 1+): $24.95, Kids Under 1: FREE, Members: FREE, Military (Active/Retired/Veteran): FREE with ID, PlayMakers (Maryland EBT/WIC): $5.00 (Port Discovery)
highlights: 'SkyClimber: A soaring four-story climbing structure ([Port Discovery)', 'S.S. Friendship: A giant cargo ship exhibit where kids can play captain (Port Discovery)', 'Wonders of Water: Interactive water play area including a bubble station (Port Discovery)', 'Adventure Expeditions: An immersive journey through 1920s Egypt (Port Discovery)', 'Tot Trails: A specialized play area designed for infants and toddlers (Port Discovery)', 'BGE Art Cove: A creative space for hands-on art projects and exploration (Port Discovery)']
attractions.tips: Advanced tickets are strongly recommended as walk-in availability is very limited. The museum is a certified sensory inclusive facility and offers Sensory Friendly Sundays. Discounted parking is available at the adjacent Harbor Park Garage (Port Discovery).
attractions.nearby: National Aquarium, Inner Harbor, Power Plant Live!, Maryland Science Center, Historic Ships in Baltimore (Sunshine Whispers, Wikipedia))

attractions.name: Reginald F. Lewis Museum of Maryland African American History & Culture
attractions.description: The Reginald F. Lewis Museum is the authentic voice of African American art, history, and culture in Maryland, featuring a collection that spans 400 years with over 11,000 artifacts. Located in downtown Baltimore, it is the largest museum of its kind in the state and serves as a premier resource for information and inspiration about the lives of African American Marylanders Official Website, Wikipedia.
attractions.hours: Mon, Thu-Sat: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sun: 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Tue-Wed: Closed Official Visit Page
attractions.admission: General Admission: $15, Seniors (65+): $12, Youth (7-17): $12, Veterans/Military: $12, Children 6 and under: FREE, Members: FREE, Maryland Public School Educators: FREE. Also participates in Museums for All (free for SNAP EBT cardholders) Official Visit Page
highlights: 'Permanent Exhibition Gallery (3rd Floor) covering family, community, and labor', 'TITAN | The Legacy of Reginald F. Lewis (special exhibition)', 'iWITNESS: Media & The Movement exhibit', 'Lynching in Maryland (permanent exhibition)', 'KID ZONE: Lifting As We Climb for family-friendly learning', '200-seat theater auditorium and outdoor terrace for special events [Official Website']
attractions.tips: It is recommended to purchase tickets online in advance to reserve your time. Visitors should start their tour on the third floor and work their way down. Validated parking is available at the PMI Garage across the street ($9) or the Pier V Garage ($7) Official Visit Page, Tripadvisor.
attractions.nearby: National Aquarium, Maryland Science Center, Phoenix Shot Tower, Star-Spangled Banner Flag House, Port Discovery Children's Museum Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Station North Arts and Entertainment District
attractions.category: Arts & Entertainment District Station North Arts District
attractions.description: Station North Arts and Entertainment District is Baltimore’s first designated arts district, spanning 104 acres across three neighborhoods. Known for its DIY spirit and vibrant public art, it serves as a creative hub for artists, makers, and performers with over 60 unique venues including galleries, theaters, and studios. Station North Arts District, Visit Baltimore
attractions.hours: Public spaces accessible 24/7; business and venue hours vary by location. Central Baltimore Partnership (office): Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. Station North Arts District, Visit Maryland
attractions.admission: Free Station North Arts District
highlights: ['Graffiti Alley (legal street art corridor)', 'SNF Parkway Theatre (historic cinema)', 'Motor House (creative incubator and performance space)', "Artscape (America's largest free arts festival)", 'Open Walls Baltimore (international mural project)', 'Station North Tool Library']
attractions.tips: Visit during the Second Friday Art Walk (5-9 PM) to experience open galleries and special events. The district is highly accessible via public transit; consider taking the MARC or Amtrak to Penn Station, which is located directly within the district. Be sure to explore the numerous murals part of the Open Walls Baltimore project throughout the neighborhood. Station North Arts District, Visit Baltimore
attractions.nearby: Penn Station, Maryland Institute College of Art (MICA), Mount Vernon neighborhood, The Walters Art Museum, University of Baltimore. Visit Baltimore, Wikipedia

attractions.name: The Maryland Zoo in Baltimore
attractions.description: Founded in 1876, the Maryland Zoo in Baltimore is the third oldest zoo in the United States and a non-profit organization dedicated to wildlife conservation. It is home to over 1,500 animals across 200 species, featuring award-winning natural habitat exhibits like Penguin Coast and African Journey. Visit Baltimore, The Maryland Zoo
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (March–December); Fri–Mon: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (January–February). Last entry is at 3:30 PM. The Maryland Zoo
attractions.days_open: Daily (March–December); Friday–Monday (January–February). Closed on Thanksgiving and Christmas Day. The Maryland Zoo
attractions.admission: General Admission (Ages 2-64): $33; Senior Admission (Ages 65+): $28; ZooMore+ (Ages 5+): $41 (includes VR ride); Children under 2: Free; Winter Admission (Jan-Feb): $24 for ages 2+. All tickets include unlimited train and carousel rides. The Maryland Zoo
highlights: ['Penguin Coast: Home to a large colony of African black-footed penguins with underwater viewing.', 'African Journey: Features iconic wildlife including elephants, lions, rhinos, giraffes, and chimpanzees.', 'Maryland Wilderness: Showcases native species such as river otters, bobcats, and a petting farmyard.', "Giraffe Feeding: An interactive experience where guests can hand-feed the zoo's reticulated giraffes.", 'The Zephyr Train and Carousel: Classic rides included with admission that offer a scenic tour of the zoo.', 'Animal Encounters: Special ticketed opportunities for close-up experiences with penguins, rhinos, and otters.']
attractions.tips: Parking is free for all guests. Visiting on Tuesdays, Wednesdays, or Thursdays typically offers smaller crowds. Strollers are highly recommended due to the zoo's size and terrain. Guests are welcome to bring their own food for a picnic on the grounds. Sunshine Whispers, The Maryland Zoo
attractions.nearby: Druid Hill Park, National Aquarium, Maryland Science Center, B&O Railroad Museum, Fort McHenry National Monument. Visit Maryland

attractions.name: USS Constellation (Sloop-of-War)
attractions.description: The USS Constellation is a sloop-of-war and the last sail-only warship designed and built by the United States Navy. Launched in 1854, she served as the flagship of the Africa Squadron and is now a National Historic Landmark preserved in Baltimore's Inner Harbor. Historic Ships in Baltimore
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (April 4 - Labor Day); Mon-Fri: 11:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Sat-Sun: 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Winter/Spring schedule varies, check website). Historic Ships in Baltimore Hours
attractions.days_open: Daily (April - Labor Day); Thursday - Monday (Winter season, check website for details) Historic Ships in Baltimore Hours
attractions.admission: Boarding Pass (All Ships): Adult (21-59) $21.95, Senior (60+)/Military $19.95, Student (15-20 or with student ID) $19.95, Youth (6-14) $9.95, Child (under 5) Free, Members Free. Historic Ships in Baltimore Tickets
highlights: 'Last sail-only warship designed and built by the U.S. Navy. [Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'Served as flagship of the U.S. Africa Squadron, capturing slave ships and freeing over 700 Africans. American Battlefield Trust', 'Designated as a National Historic Landmark and the only surviving ship from the Civil War era. Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'Four distinct decks to explore, including the gun deck with interactive Parrott rifle drills. Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'Extensive artifact collection containing approximately 50,000 maritime objects and documents. Historic Ships in Baltimore']
attractions.tips: The museum and gift shop are cashless; only card payments are accepted. Wear comfortable, flat-soled shoes as the historic decks and ladders can be steep and uneven. A single boarding pass provides access to all four historic ships and the lighthouse. Historic Ships in Baltimore Tickets
attractions.nearby: National Aquarium, Maryland Science Center, USS Torsk, USCGC Taney, Seven Foot Knoll Lighthouse, Port Discovery Children's Museum. Visit Maryland

attractions.name: USS Torsk Submarine (SS-423)
attractions.description: The USS Torsk (SS-423) is a Tench-class submarine commissioned for the United States Navy in 1944. It is notable for sinking the last enemy warships of World War II and now serves as a museum and memorial in Baltimore's Inner Harbor. Wikipedia, Historic Ships in Baltimore
attractions.address: Pier 3, Baltimore's Inner Harbor, 301 E Pratt St.
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Last boarding at 4:30 PM). Note: Schedules vary seasonally; typically closed Tue/Wed during winter months. Historic Ships in Baltimore
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Tuesdays and Wednesdays in most of the off-season, but currently open Daily during the spring/summer season) Historic Ships in Baltimore
attractions.admission: Adult (21-59): $21.95, Senior (60+)/Military: $19.95, Student (15-20 or with student ID): $19.95, Youth (6-14): $9.95, Child (under 5): Free, Members: Free. Historic Ships in Baltimore
highlights: 'The last U.S. Navy ship to sink enemy vessels in World War II [Wikipedia', 'Tour of the torpedo rooms, engine room, and crew mess areas Historic Ships in Baltimore', 'Designated as a National Historic Landmark in 1986 Maryland Historical Trust', 'Participated in the blockade of Cuba during the Cuban Missile Crisis Wikipedia', 'Active overnight adventure programs for groups and youth USS Torsk Volunteer Association']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes suitable for climbing ladders and navigating tight spaces. The ship is not cash-friendly; all tickets and gift shop purchases must be made by card. All visitors under 17 must be accompanied by an adult. Historic Ships in Baltimore, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: National Aquarium, LV116 Chesapeake Lightship, USS Constellation, USCG Cutter 37, Seven Foot Knoll Lighthouse, World Trade Center Baltimore. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: United States lightship Chesapeake (LS-116/WAL-538/WLV-538)
attractions.description: The Lightship Chesapeake is a historic museum ship that served as a floating lighthouse at the entrance of the Chesapeake Bay from 1930 to 1971. Built with a steel hull and equipped with powerful electric lamps, it was considered a marvel of engineering in its time and survived powerful hurricanes while remaining on station. Today, it is part of the Historic Ships in Baltimore collection, offering visitors an immersive look at the challenging lives of the sailors who manned these vital navigational aids (Wikipedia), National Park Service).
attractions.address: Pier 3, 301 E Pratt St, Baltimore, MD 21202
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Last boarding at 4:30 PM) (Historic Ships in Baltimore)
attractions.admission: Adult (21-59): $21.95, Senior (60+)/Military: $19.95, Student (15-20 or with student ID): $19.95, Youth (6-14): $9.95, Child (under 5): Free, Members: Free, Active duty/reservist/retired military: Free (Historic Ships in Baltimore)
highlights: ['Designated as a National Historic Landmark in 1980 for its historical significance.', 'Best-preserved example of a third-generation American lightship.', 'Equipped with two 5,000-pound mushroom anchors designed to withstand extreme storms.', 'Served as a military patrol vessel during World War II near the Cape Cod Canal.', "Features well-restored interior spaces including the crew's mess, staterooms, and engine room."]
attractions.tips: Wear flat, comfortable shoes for navigating the ship's decks and ladders. The museum is cashless, so ensure you have a card for ticket and gift shop purchases. Purchase a Boarding Pass if you plan to visit multiple historic vessels in the harbor to save on individual admission costs (Historic Ships in Baltimore, Expedia).
attractions.nearby: USS Torsk (Submarine), National Aquarium, USS Constellation, USCG Cutter 37, Seven Foot Knoll Lighthouse, Maryland Science Center (Historic Ships in Baltimore)

attractions.name: Canton Neighborhood
attractions.description: Canton is a vibrant waterfront community in East Baltimore known for its historic rowhouses, active social scene, and scenic harbor views. Traditionally centered around O'Donnell Square, the neighborhood offers a unique mix of urban and nautical charm with plenty of local bars, restaurants, and green spaces. Canton Community Association, Live Baltimore
attractions.address: 3500 Boston St, Suite 235 (Canton Community Association HQ) / 3001 Boston St (Canton Waterfront Park)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Always open (24/7); Canton Waterfront Park: Open daily from dusk to dawn Canton Community Association
attractions.admission: Free Canton Community Association
highlights: ["O'Donnell Square Park: A historic village square and the neighborhood's social heart.", 'Canton Waterfront Park: Eight acres of green space featuring harbor views and a boat ramp.', 'Maryland Korean War Memorial: A significant landmark located within the waterfront park.', 'Baltimore Promenade: A scenic waterfront walkway connecting Canton to the Inner Harbor.', 'Historic Rowhouses: Quintessential Baltimore architecture with marble stoops and painted screens.', 'Community Events: Hosts popular festivals like WTMD’s First Thursdays and the Baltimore Seafood Fest.']
attractions.tips: Street parking is often limited; consider using the Baltimore Water Taxi or public transit. Be mindful of monthly street sweeping schedules (first and second Wednesdays) to avoid parking tickets. The waterfront park is a premier spot for watching harbor sunsets and airshows. Canton Community Association, Niche
attractions.nearby: Patterson Park, Fells Point, Highlandtown, Brewers Hill, Fort McHenry Homes.com, Visit Baltimore

attractions.name: Fells Point Historic District
attractions.description: Fells Point is a vibrant historic waterfront neighborhood established in 1763, renowned for its well-preserved 18th- and 19th-century architecture and Belgian block cobblestone streets. Once a bustling shipbuilding hub where the famous Baltimore Clippers were built, it now serves as a lively cultural center filled with unique boutiques, over 120 pubs, and scenic harbor views (Visit Baltimore).
attractions.hours: District: Open 24 hours daily. Visitor Center (Apr-Dec): Tue-Thu 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Fri 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM; (Jan-Mar): Tue-Fri 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Sat 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Sun 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Wikivoyage).
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Maryland)
highlights: "Belgian Block Streets: Iconic 18th-century cobblestone streets that define the district's maritime character ([Airial Travel).", 'Broadway Market: A historic indoor market built in 1786, featuring a variety of international and local delicacies (Visit Baltimore).', 'Frederick Douglass-Isaac Myers Maritime Park: A museum and heritage site honoring African American maritime history (Living Classrooms).', 'Waterfront Promenade: A scenic walking path along the harbor offering stunning views of the city and water (FellsPoint.com).', "The Horse You Came In On Saloon: Baltimore's oldest bar, famously known as Edgar Allan Poe's rumored last stop (Visit Baltimore).", 'Fells Point Farmers Market: A popular Saturday morning market for local produce and artisanal goods (Airial Travel).']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes to navigate the charming but uneven Belgian block streets. Parking can be difficult on weekends, so consider using a parking garage or taking the Baltimore Water Taxi for a scenic arrival (Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: Inner Harbor, National Aquarium, Harbor East, Canton, Domino Sugar Factory (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Little Italy Baltimore
attractions.description: Little Italy is a historic and charming ethnic enclave in Baltimore, renowned for its strong Italian-American heritage and abundance of authentic family-owned restaurants. Nestled between the Inner Harbor and Fells Point, it features narrow cobblestone streets, traditional row houses, and a vibrant community atmosphere centered around cultural landmarks like St. Leo the Great Church.
attractions.address: Stiles & High Streets, Baltimore, MD 21202 (Central coordinates)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Neighborhood); Most restaurants open daily Noon - Midnight
attractions.admission: Free (Neighborhood access; individual attractions and restaurants have varying prices)
highlights: ['Authentic Italian dining at over 30 family-owned restaurants and bakeries.', 'Annual summer events including the Open Air Film Fest and Feast of St. Anthony.', 'Historic St. Leo the Great Roman Catholic Church, established in 1881.', "Public bocce ball courts at D'Alesandro Park for traditional community play.", 'The Pandola Learning Center offering Italian language and cooking classes.', 'Proximity to the Inner Harbor and President Street Station Civil War Museum.']
attractions.tips: Visit during the summer for the Open Air Film Fest on Friday nights or attend one of the major Italian festivals in June and August. Free street parking is available but limited to 3 hours; several paid garages are located on the neighborhood's perimeter for longer stays. Many restaurants offer valet parking or complimentary shuttles from downtown.
attractions.nearby: Inner Harbor, Fells Point, National Aquarium, Harbor East, President Street Station Civil War Museum, Star-Spangled Banner Flag House.

attractions.name: Star-Spangled Banner Flag House & Museum
attractions.description: The Star-Spangled Banner Flag House is a National Historic Landmark and historic house museum dedicated to the story of Mary Young Pickersgill. In 1813, Pickersgill, along with her family and assistants, sewed the massive 30-by-42-foot garrison flag that flew over Fort McHenry during the Battle of Baltimore, later inspiring the U.S. national anthem. (Official Website) (National Park Service)
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Friday: 10 AM – 3 PM; Saturday: 10 AM – 4 PM; Sunday & Monday: Closed (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults: $9, Seniors & Military: $8, Students (K-12): $7, Children under 6: Free (Official Website)
highlights: 'Visit the 1793 historic home of Mary Pickersgill furnished to its 1813 appearance ([Official Website)', "Explore the 'Family of Flagmakers' exhibit focused on the women who created the Star-Spangled Banner (Official Website)", "Interactive Children's Discovery Gallery where kids can design flags and 'cook' in a replica kitchen (TheDistrict.com)", 'View a 10-minute orientation film detailing the history of the flag and the War of 1812 (Tripadvisor)', 'Pleasant courtyard gardens featuring a mosaic stone map of the United States (Tripadvisor)', 'Large museum window designed to the exact size and scale of the original Star-Spangled Banner (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Tours of the historic house are currently self-guided, though docent-led tours may be available on specific days. Check the online events calendar for holiday closures and special programming. Limited free street parking is available on Albemarle Street, and a parking garage is located across Pratt Street. (Official Website) (Baltimore Museums)
attractions.nearby: Little Italy, Inner Harbor, National Aquarium, Fort McHenry National Monument, Port Discovery Children's Museum (Visit Baltimore) (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Federal Hill Park
attractions.description: Federal Hill Park is a 10.3-acre historic landmark on the south shore of Baltimore's Inner Harbor, offering iconic panoramic views of the downtown skyline (Wikipedia). Originally a defensive stronghold during the War of 1812 and a Union fortification during the Civil War, it is now a signature public space for recreation, photography, and community events (Visit Baltimore).
attractions.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Thursday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Friday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Saturday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sunday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Panoramic views of the Inner Harbor and downtown skyline ([Yelp)', "Historic-themed children's playground featuring a B&O railroad engine (SHR for Federal Hill Park)", 'Civil War era cannons and monuments to War of 1812 heroes (Wikipedia)', 'Flag Staff Plaza and scenic walking paths (SHR for Federal Hill Park)', 'Public basketball courts renovated by Under Armour (Wikipedia)', 'A premier location for watching city fireworks and stargazing (Holidify)']
attractions.tips: The park offers some of the best sunset views in the city, making it a popular spot for photography (Yelp). Street parking is available around the perimeter but can be difficult to find; paid parking garages at the Inner Harbor are a reliable alternative (Yelp). Please note there are no public restrooms on the hill itself, though facilities are available nearby at Rash Field (Yelp).
attractions.nearby: American Visionary Art Museum, Rash Field, Maryland Science Center, Inner Harbor, Baltimore Museum of Industry, Cross Street Market (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Fort McHenry National Monument and Historic Shrine National Park Service
attractions.category: Landmark, National Monument, and Historic Shrine National Park Service, Heritage Foundation
attractions.description: Fort McHenry is a historical coastal pentagonal bastion fort best known for its pivotal role in the War of 1812. Its successful defense of Baltimore Harbor from a British naval attack in 1814 inspired Francis Scott Key to pen the lyrics for "The Star-Spangled Banner," which later became the U.S. national anthem National Park Service, Wikipedia. Today, it serves as the only designated National Monument and Historic Shrine in the United States Heritage Foundation.
attractions.address: 2400 East Fort Avenue National Park Service
attractions.hours: Park Grounds & Parking: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily; Historic Star Fort & Visitor Center: 9:00 AM – 4:45 PM daily; Last entry: 4:45 PM National Park Service
attractions.days_open: Daily (closed Thanksgiving, December 25, and January 1) National Park Service
attractions.admission: Adults (16+): $15.00 for a 7-day pass; Children (15 and under): Free; Park Grounds: Free for recreational use National Park Service, Visit Baltimore
highlights: 'Touring the Historic Star Fort to see original 19th-century buildings and bastions [National Park Service', 'Participating in daily Flag Change Programs to fold and raise replica garrison flags National Park Service', 'Watching the 10-minute orientation film on the Battle of Baltimore at the Visitor Center Visit Baltimore', 'Exploring exhibits of war artifacts and historical military equipment Visit Baltimore', 'Attending ranger-led educational talks and historic weapons demonstrations National Park Service', 'Completing activities for the Junior Ranger Program National Park Service']
attractions.tips: The park is completely cashless, so bring a credit or debit card for the entrance fee National Park Service. Visitors are encouraged to purchase passes online in advance via recreation.gov to save time National Park Service. Pets are permitted in the park grounds on a leash but are restricted from the historic fort buildings Visit Baltimore.
attractions.nearby: Antietam National Battlefield, Gettysburg National Military Park, Hampton National Historic Site, Baltimore Inner Harbor National Park Service

attractions.name: Leone-Riverside Park
attractions.description: Leone-Riverside Park is a 17-acre historic community park in South Baltimore, established in 1875 and recognized as a site on the Star-Spangled Banner National Historic Trail. It features an iconic marble pavilion, panoramic views of the Patapsco River, and the Congressman Elijah E. Cummings Community Pool, serving as a vibrant green space for the surrounding Riverside neighborhood (Riverside Neighborhood Association, Baltimore Heritage).
attractions.hours: Park: Monday–Sunday, Dawn to Dusk (ParkRx America). Pool (Seasonal): Tuesday–Thursday, 12:00 PM – 3:00 PM and 4:00 PM – 7:00 PM; Friday–Sunday, 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM and 4:00 PM – 8:00 PM; Closed Mondays (CBS News, WMAR 2 News).
attractions.admission: Park: Free; Pool: $2.00 per person (Yelp, Holidify)
highlights: ['Historic marble pavilion (restored in 2024)', 'Congressman Elijah E. Cummings Community Pool', 'Designated Star-Spangled Banner Trail site with War of 1812 history', 'Panoramic views of the South Baltimore peninsula and Patapsco River', 'Athletic facilities including baseball fields, basketball courts, and soccer fields', "Fenced-in children's playground and shaded walking paths"]
attractions.tips: Street parking is available around the park, but be mindful of signs as some areas are restricted to 2-hour limits without a residential permit. The pool typically requires a CivicRec account for entry, so it is recommended to set one up in advance (Tripadvisor, WMAR 2 News).
attractions.nearby: LP Steamers (Seafood), Federal Hill Park, American Visionary Art Museum, Inner Harbor, Fort McHenry National Monument (Tripadvisor, Tinyhood)

attractions.name: Baltimore Streetcar Museum, Inc.
attractions.description: The Baltimore Streetcar Museum is a non-profit educational and cultural resource dedicated to preserving the history of public transportation in Baltimore, specifically the electric streetcar era. Visitors can enjoy unlimited rides on a variety of meticulously restored vintage streetcars along a scenic loop in the Jones Falls valley. The museum features an extensive collection of vehicles dating back to 1859, a research library, and interactive exhibits detailing the city's transit evolution (Official Website, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Spring (Mar 1 – May 17): Sun 12 PM – 5 PM; Summer (May 23 – Aug 30): Sat & Sun 11 AM – 5 PM; Summer Wednesdays (Jun 3 – Aug 26): Wed 10 AM – 3 PM; Fall (Sep 1 – Dec 1): Sun 12 PM – 5 PM; Winter: Closed Jan & Feb (Official Website).
attractions.days_open: Sundays (Spring/Fall); Saturdays, Sundays, and Wednesdays (Summer) (Official Website).
attractions.admission: Adults: $20.00; Children (ages 4-11): $15.00; Children under 4: Free. All tickets include unlimited streetcar rides for the day (Official Website).
highlights: 'Car 417 (1888): Reported to be the oldest operating electric streetcar in America ([Official Website).', 'Car 7407 (1944): A Pullman PCC car that was the last streetcar to turn a revenue wheel in Baltimore in 1963 (Official Website).', 'Car 25 (1859): A first-year horse car from the Baltimore City Passenger Railway and the oldest vehicle in the collection (Wikipedia).', 'Car 554 (1896): A beautifully restored 9-bench open summer car (Official Website).', 'Car C-145 (1923): A massive snow sweeper used to keep Baltimore’s transit lines clear during winter storms (Official Website).', 'Maryland Rail Heritage Library: Houses significant collections and archives of transit history from the NRHS and the museum (Official Website).']
attractions.tips: Admission includes unlimited rides, so take the opportunity to experience several different vintage cars. The museum is located in a valley below the Howard Street Bridge; entrance is off Falls Road just north of Penn Station. Wear comfortable shoes for boarding the historic rolling stock and check the seasonal calendar for special events like 'Santa’s Streetcar' (Tripadvisor, Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Jones Falls Trail, Parkway Theatre, Baltimore Penn Station, Baltimore Museum of Art, The Walters Art Museum (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Enoch Pratt Free Library - Central Library
attractions.description: The Enoch Pratt Free Library is one of the oldest free public library systems in the United States, serving as a vital cultural and educational hub in Baltimore. Its Central Library is a beautifully restored historic landmark that provides access to extensive print collections, specialized research departments, and modern technology. As the Maryland State Library Resource Center, it offers unparalleled resources and diverse community programs to all Maryland citizens (Enoch Pratt Free Library, Visit Baltimore).
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: CLOSED (Enoch Pratt Free Library)
attractions.admission: Free (Enoch Pratt Free Library, Visit Baltimore)
highlights: 'Designated as the Maryland State Library Resource Center (SLRC) since 1971 ([Enoch Pratt Free Library).', 'Home to significant Special Collections, including the Edgar Allan Poe and H.L. Mencken rooms (Visit Baltimore).', 'Features the Hackerman Artist & Writer in Residence Programs that bring local creatives to the library (Enoch Pratt Free Library).', 'Offers comprehensive community services such as a Job & Career Information Center, social workers, and legal assistance (Enoch Pratt Free Library).', "Provides free weekly public tours that offer a behind-the-scenes look at the library's history and stacks (Enoch Pratt Free Library).", 'Houses diverse specialized departments including African American, Fine Arts & Music, and Maryland departments (Enoch Pratt Free Library).']
attractions.tips: Appointments are strongly recommended for those wishing to use the Special Collections for research purposes (Enoch Pratt Free Library). Taking a free public tour is the best way to see restricted areas like the stacks and learn about the building's historic restoration (Tripadvisor). Visitors also suggest using the restrooms on the second floor for a better experience (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Baltimore Basilica, The Walters Art Museum, Washington Monument, George Peabody Library, Maryland Center for History and Culture (Tripadvisor, St. Mary's Paca Street)

attractions.name: George Peabody Library
attractions.description: The George Peabody Library is a world-renowned research library and one of the most beautiful interior spaces in Baltimore. Designed by architect Edmund G. Lind and opened in 1878, it features five tiers of ornamental cast-iron balconies that rise to a massive skylight 61 feet above the floor. Today, it serves as part of the Sheridan Libraries of Johns Hopkins University and remains free and open to the public. (Sheridan Libraries, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Monday – Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Friday – Saturday: Closed (Sheridan Libraries)
attractions.admission: Free (Sheridan Libraries)
highlights: 'Five-tier stack room with ornamental cast-iron balconies ([Sheridan Libraries)', 'Dramatic 61-foot-high skylight (Sheridan Libraries)', 'Collection of 300,000 volumes, mainly from the 19th century (Sheridan Libraries)', 'Part of the Johns Hopkins University Sheridan Libraries (Sheridan Libraries)', 'Commonly referred to as the "Cathedral of Books" (A Well-Read Wanderer)']
attractions.tips: Admission is free, but access is limited to the ground floor of the stack room. Casual, handheld photography for personal use is permitted, though tripods and flash are prohibited. Visitors should check the official website for current hours as they may change for private events or holidays. (Sheridan Libraries, A Well-Read Wanderer)
attractions.nearby: Peabody Institute of The Johns Hopkins University, The Walters Art Museum, Washington Monument and Mount Vernon Place, Maryland Center for History and Culture (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Maryland Center for History and Culture (formerly Maryland Historical Society)
attractions.description: The Maryland Center for History and Culture (MCHC) is the oldest continuously operating nonprofit cultural institution in the state, collecting and preserving Maryland's diverse heritage. It features a museum with 23,000 square feet of gallery space and a research library housing 7 million books and documents.
attractions.hours: Wednesday–Sunday: 10 am – 5 pm (Extended until 8 pm on First Thursdays)
attractions.admission: $12 Adults, $10 Seniors, $9 Students, FREE for Members and Children 5 and under. First Thursday of every month is free from 5 pm to 8 pm.
highlights: ['The world’s largest collection of Baltimore Album quilts', 'Nine portraits by Joshua Johnson, the first professional African American artist in the U.S.', 'The world’s largest assembly of paintings by the Peale family', 'A fashion archive containing 12,000 garments and accessories spanning four centuries', 'The original manuscript of the "Star-Spangled Banner" (located in the museum)', 'Over 350,000 museum objects and 7 million library items reflecting Maryland history']
attractions.tips: It is strongly encouraged to book tickets in advance due to hourly capacity limits. Library research in Special Collections requires an appointment. Enjoy free admission on the first Thursday of each month during extended evening hours.
attractions.nearby: The Walters Art Museum, Washington Monument (Mount Vernon), Enoch Pratt Free Library, Baltimore Basilica, Peabody Library, Hotel Revival, Dooby’s Cafe.

attractions.name: Mount Vernon Neighborhood / Mount Vernon Place Historic District
attractions.description: Mount Vernon is considered Baltimore's cultural center, known for its well-preserved 19th-century architecture and as the home of the original Washington Monument. This hip and elegant historic district is filled with grand old mansions, world-class museums, shops, and restaurants, retaining its original charm while serving as a centerpiece of the city's cultural life (Source: Visit Baltimore, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Sunday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Monument); Parks: Open daily from dawn to dusk (Source: Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday - Sunday (for Monument interior); Daily (for Parks) (Source: Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)
attractions.admission: Gallery Level: Free; Lookout Level (climb): $6 for adults, $4 for children (Source: Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)
attractions.phone: 410-962-5070 (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy) (Source: Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)
highlights: ['Washington Monument: The first monument dedicated to George Washington in the United States, offering panoramic city views from its lookout.', 'Walters Art Museum: An internationally renowned museum featuring an extensive collection of art from pre-dynastic Egypt to 20th-century Europe.', 'Peabody Institute: The oldest conservatory in the United States, known for its stunning George Peabody Library.', "Maryland Center for History and Culture: Home to the original manuscript of the 'Star-Spangled Banner' and extensive regional history exhibits.", 'Mount Vernon Place Parks: Four beautiful urban squares surrounding the monument, designed by the firms of Olmsted and Carrère & Hastings.', 'Enoch Pratt Free Library: The historic central branch of Baltimore’s public library system, one of the first free libraries in the nation.']
attractions.tips: Purchase climb tickets for the Washington Monument online in advance, especially on weekends, as they are limited to 5 people every 20 minutes. Note that the monument has no elevator, heat, air conditioning, or restrooms. (Source: Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)
attractions.nearby: The Walters Art Museum, Peabody Institute, Maryland Center for History & Culture, Enoch Pratt Free Library, Baltimore School for the Arts, Meyerhoff Symphony Hall (Source: Visit Baltimore)

attractions.name: Mount Vernon Place
attractions.category: Park, Landmark, and Historic District (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)
attractions.description: Mount Vernon Place is a National Historic Landmark District in Baltimore featuring the first monument dedicated to George Washington in the United States. The site consists of four elegantly designed park squares—North, South, East, and West—surrounded by some of the city's most significant cultural institutions and historic architecture. Visitors can explore the interactive gallery at the base of the monument or climb its 227 marble steps for panoramic views of the city (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy, Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Monument: Wed–Sun, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Parks: Daily, Dawn to Dusk (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy, Yelp)
attractions.days_open: Monument: Wednesday–Sunday; Parks: Daily (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy, Yelp)
attractions.admission: Gallery Level: Free; Lookout Level (Climb): $6 for Adults, $4 for Children (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)
highlights: 'Home to the first monument dedicated to George Washington in the United States ([Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)', 'Four historic park squares (North, South, East, and West) featuring fountains and statuary (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)', 'The 178-foot Washington Monument with a 227-step spiral staircase to the lookout (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)', "The Gallery at the monument's base featuring interactive digital exhibits on the site's history (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)", 'Host of annual community events such as the Baltimore Flower Mart and the Monument Lighting (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy)']
attractions.tips: Climb tickets should be purchased online in advance during busy summer months or weekends; wear comfortable shoes for the 227-step climb. Note that the monument has no air conditioning or restrooms (Mount Vernon Place Conservancy). Visit on a clear day for the best 360-degree views from the lookout level (Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Walters Art Museum, George Peabody Library, Enoch Pratt Free Library, Maryland Center for History and Culture (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Peabody Institute of the Johns Hopkins University
attractions.description: The Peabody Institute of the Johns Hopkins University is the oldest conservatory in the United States, founded in 1857 as a community cultural center. It is a world-renowned music and dance school that stages over 1,000 concerts and events annually, blending historic architecture with a vibrant artistic community. Peabody Institute, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: George Peabody Library: Mon–Thu 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Fri–Sun Closed). Peabody Garage: Mon–Fri 6:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Sat 7:30 AM – 5:30 PM, Sun 12:30 PM – 5:30 PM. Concert times vary (typically 7:30 PM for main stage events). Sheridan Libraries, Official Site
attractions.days_open: Library: Monday–Thursday; Conservatory/Concerts: Daily (schedule varies by performance)
attractions.admission: Free for most performances; certain special events or non-student performances may require paid tickets (see website for specific event pricing). George Peabody Library is free and open to the public. Visit Maryland, Official Site
highlights: 'George Peabody Library, a stunning 19th-century "cathedral of books" with five tiers of cast-iron balconies. [Visit Baltimore', "The Peabody Conservatory, America's first music conservatory and a top-tier institution for music and dance. Peabody Institute", 'Over 1,000 annual performances, many of which are free student recitals and ensemble concerts open to the public. Official Site', 'BREAKTHROUGH Curriculum, an innovative program preparing artists for 21st-century careers in the performing arts. Visit Baltimore', 'Mount Vernon Place campus, a National Historic Landmark District featuring classically inspired 19th-century architecture. Library of Congress']
attractions.tips: Many performances are free but may require advance registration; check the online calendar frequently as schedules can change. The George Peabody Library allows casual photography but is a quiet study space; professional shoots must be pre-arranged for Fridays. Parking is available in the Peabody Garage, but street parking is limited in the historic district. Official Site, Sheridan Libraries
attractions.nearby: Washington Monument and Mount Vernon Place, The Walters Art Museum, Maryland Center for History and Culture, Baltimore Center Stage, Basilica of the Assumption. Visit Baltimore

attractions.name: The Walters Art Museum
attractions.description: The Walters Art Museum is among America’s most distinctive museums, forging connections between people and art from cultures around the world and spanning seven millennia. Located in Baltimore’s Mount Vernon neighborhood, the museum’s campus includes five historic buildings and a collection of 36,000 art objects. The Walters Art Museum, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Wednesday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m.; Thursday: 1 p.m.–8 p.m.; Friday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m.; Saturday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m.; Sunday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m.; Monday–Tuesday: Closed The Walters Art Museum
attractions.admission: Free for everyone (includes special exhibitions) The Walters Art Museum, Wikipedia
highlights: ['18th and 19th-century French paintings', 'Ethiopian icons and richly illuminated manuscripts', 'Ancient Roman sarcophagi and Greek sculpture', "The Chamber of Art & Wonders (17th-century scholar's library)", 'Hall of Arms & Armor featuring medieval weaponry', 'The Conservation Window where visitors can see conservators at work']
attractions.tips: Visit on Thursday evenings for extended hours and live programming. Use the complimentary lockers to store bags and water bottles, as food and drink are prohibited in galleries. Check the top floor for the Conservation Window to see live art restoration. The Walters Art Museum, Scholarly Skater
attractions.nearby: Washington Monument, Mount Vernon Place Park, Peabody Institute, Maryland Center for History and Culture Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Washington Monument (Baltimore)
attractions.description: The Washington Monument in Baltimore is a historic 178-foot Doric column completed in 1829, serving as the first major commemorative structure dedicated to George Washington in the United States. Located in the heart of the Mount Vernon neighborhood, the monument features a restored white marble interior with a gallery of interactive digital exhibits and a 227-step stair tower leading to a scenic lookout level. Mount Vernon Place Conservancy, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Closed Monday, Tuesday, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day. Mount Vernon Place Conservancy, Visit Baltimore
attractions.admission: Gallery Level: Free; Lookout Level (climb): $6 for Adults, $4 for Children (up to age 13) Mount Vernon Place Conservancy, Visit Baltimore
highlights: 'Interactive digital exhibits in the Gallery providing virtual tours and history. [Mount Vernon Place Conservancy', 'Climbing the 227 historic marble steps to the lookout level. Visit Baltimore', 'Panoramic views of Baltimore and the historic Mount Vernon neighborhood from the top. Visit Baltimore', 'Architecture designed by Robert Mills, who also designed the later Washington Monument in D.C. Wikipedia)', 'Historic centerpiece of Mount Vernon Place, a National Historic Landmark District. Visit Maryland', 'Annual Monument Lighting ceremony held every December. Downtown Partnership of Baltimore']
attractions.tips: Purchase climb tickets in advance online or on-site (climbs occur every 20 minutes); the monument lacks heating/air conditioning, so dress for the weather; no restrooms are available inside the monument; large bags or backpacks are not permitted. Mount Vernon Place Conservancy
attractions.nearby: The Walters Art Museum, George Peabody Library, Enoch Pratt Free Library, Maryland Center for History and Culture, Basilica of the National Shrine of the Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary. TripAdvisor, Visit Baltimore

attractions.name: Hampden Neighborhood
attractions.description: Hampden is a vibrant and historic neighborhood in northern Baltimore known for its quirky, kitschy charm and its bustling commercial strip known as "The Avenue." Originally settled as a 19th-century mill village, the area has transformed into a creative hub filled with independent boutiques, art galleries, and some of the city's most acclaimed restaurants. (Wikipedia, Visit Baltimore)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Typical Business Hours: Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Individual store and restaurant hours vary) (Yelp)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Maryland)
highlights: '"The Avenue" (West 36th Street) shopping and dining district ([Visit Baltimore)', 'Miracle on 34th Street holiday lights display (Living in Maryland)', 'Annual HonFest and Hampdenfest community festivals (Visit Baltimore)', 'Atomic Books, a legendary indie bookstore (DC Travel Magazine)', 'Union Collective maker space and brewery (Visit Baltimore)', 'Baltimore Streetcar Museum (Hampden Merchants)']
attractions.tips: Explore the neighborhood on foot to fully experience the unique storefronts and murals along "The Avenue." If visiting on a weekend, consider using rideshares or the light rail (Woodberry Station) as street parking can be limited. Don't miss the holiday lights on 34th Street if visiting in December. (DC Travel Magazine, Allie Marie Travels)
attractions.nearby: Druid Hill Park, Maryland Zoo in Baltimore, Baltimore Museum of Art, Johns Hopkins University Homewood Campus (Wikipedia, Visit Baltimore)

attractions.name: R. House Food Hall
attractions.description: R. House is a vibrant 50,000-square-foot food hall located in a former auto body shop in Baltimore's Remington neighborhood. It serves as a launchpad for 10 chef-driven stalls and a central bar, fostering a community-focused environment where neighbors can come together for diverse culinary experiences. The venue features an industrial-chic design with roll-up garage doors and a large seating area that hosts various community events and pop-ups. (Official Website, Visit Baltimore)
attractions.hours: Breakfast: Mon-Sun 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Lunch & Dinner: Sun-Thu 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM; r. bar: Mon-Thu 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sun 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Instagram)
highlights: 'Ten diverse chef-driven food stalls offering cuisines like Korean BBQ, tacos, and Egyptian street food ([Official Website)', 'A central bar (r. bar) featuring craft cocktails, local beers, and wines (Official Website)', 'The Garage, a 6,500-square-foot industrial space for private events and community pop-ups (Official Website)', "Weekend 'Brunch House' featuring specialized drink and menu options from all vendors (Official Website)", 'Vintagepalooza, a recurring pop-up clothing bazaar held in the garage space (Visit Baltimore)', 'Abundant indoor and outdoor seating with a family-friendly, communal atmosphere (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Check the event calendar for 'Vintagepalooza' markets and live performances in the Garage. Parking is available in several designated lots, including free options at the nearby police lot and validated parking for visitors. If you are visiting for coffee or breakfast, Kitsch Cafe opens early at 7 AM while the main food stalls start service at 11:30 AM. (Official Website, Visit Baltimore)
attractions.nearby: Wyman Park Dell, Baltimore Museum of Art (BMA), Clavel (Mexican restaurant), Beyond Video, Papermoon Diner, Johns Hopkins University Homewood Campus (Visit Baltimore, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: The Avenue in Hampden
attractions.description: "The Avenue" is the vibrant heart of Baltimore's Hampden neighborhood, centered along West 36th Street. This historic merchant corridor is famous for its quirky, independent boutiques, acclaimed restaurants, and the iconic "Hon" culture that embodies Baltimore's unique charm. Visitors can enjoy a mix of vintage treasures, local art, and a bustling nightlife scene within a walkable, community-focused environment (Visit Baltimore, AFAR).
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary); Tuesday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary); Wednesday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary); Thursday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary); Friday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary); Saturday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary); Sunday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary) (Visit Maryland)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Baltimore)
highlights: ['Eclectic boutiques and vintage shops', 'Award-winning restaurants and dive bars', 'Annual festivals like HonFest and Hampdenfest', 'Iconic "Hon" culture and Baltimore\'s "kitsch" aesthetic', 'Street art and vibrant murals', 'Proximity to the "Miracle on 34th Street" holiday light display']
attractions.tips: The area is best explored on foot; wear comfortable shoes. Street parking is available but limited; consider using the paid parking lot at 36th St and Roland Ave. Visit during HonFest in June or the holidays to see the famous 34th Street lights nearby (Allie Marie Travels).
attractions.nearby: Baltimore Museum of Art, Druid Hill Park, Maryland Zoo, Jones Falls Trail, Miracle on 34th Street (seasonal) (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Baltimore Museum of Art (BMA)
attractions.description: The Baltimore Museum of Art (BMA) is a major cultural institution that connects art to Baltimore and the world through a mission of excellence and equity. Founded in 1914, it houses a world-renowned collection of over 97,000 objects, including the largest public holding of works by Henri Matisse. The museum is celebrated for its historic architecture, vibrant rotating exhibitions, and commitment to making art accessible to all. Baltimore Museum of Art About, Press Release
attractions.hours: Wednesday–Sunday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m.; Thursday: Extended Hours until 9 p.m.; Monday–Tuesday: Closed. Baltimore Museum of Art Visit
attractions.admission: General admission is Free. Special exhibitions may require a separate ticket. Baltimore Museum of Art
highlights: ['The Cone Collection, featuring the world’s largest public collection of Henri Matisse works along with masterpieces by Picasso, Cezanne, and Gauguin.', 'Two stunning sculpture gardens encompassing 2.7 acres with 33 modern and contemporary works by artists like Rodin and Calder.', 'The Merrick Historic Entrance and building designed by renowned architect John Russell Pope.', "The Ruth R. Marder Center for Matisse Studies, a dedicated resource for scholars and visitors to explore Matisse's legacy.", 'A significant collection of prints, drawings, photographs, and contemporary art from diverse global perspectives.', 'Gertrude’s Chesapeake Kitchen, an on-site restaurant offering locally sourced Maryland cuisine.']
attractions.tips: General admission is free, but visitors are encouraged to reserve a free ticket online in advance. Start your visit at the Welcome Desk to pick up a map and get highlights from the Visitor Experience team. The Sculpture Gardens and Spring House are open dawn to dusk, weather permitting. Strollers are welcome, and baby-changing stations are available in every restroom. Baltimore Museum of Art Tips
attractions.nearby: Johns Hopkins University Homewood Campus, Wyman Park Dell, Sherwood Gardens, Homewood Museum, and the vibrant Charles Village neighborhood. Sherwood Gardens, Bucketlisted
